texto: Even if we're among the lucky few who benefit from civilization, we find ourselves curiously unsatisfied, plagued by stress, worry, and conflict... Like the addict who believes against all evidence that what he can't give up won't lead to suffering and death, our culture adheres to its ideas in spite of ample, clear evidence they will lead to suffering and death.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Carter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a saude mental. O trecho “Even if we're among the lucky few who benefit from civilization, we find ourselves curiously unsa…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que saude-mental, addiction, anti-civ pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e anti-civ.
